role: Hardline cleric; former MP; MOIS-linked; publicly justified chain murders
bio: Ruhollah Hosseinian is a hardline cleric who worked within the [Ministry of Intelligence](/orgs/ministry-of-intelligence) apparatus and later served as a member of parliament. He became notorious for publicly justifying the [chain murders](/events/chain-murders-iran-1998) on television, declaring: 'We have been a murderer ourselves! Murder of dissidents is not carried out the way that it has been described!' He attended Saeed Emami's memorial service. Hosseinian's open defence of the killings, at a time when the regime was attempting to portray them as the work of 'rogue elements,' was seen as revealing the true sentiment of the hardline establishment toward the assassination campaign.
editorial_notes: Born c. 1953. Hardline cleric, former MP. MOIS-linked. Publicly justified chain murders on TV. Attended Emami's memorial.
